alloc_view.summary: Allocate a new view-id and emit the corresponding stream_begin envelope. Returns the view-id as a Maxima string. @var{kind} is a renderer-side dispatch key (e.g. @code{ode_trajectory}, @code{table_append}, @code{mcmc_chain}).
alloc_view.body_md:
Allocate a new view-id and emit the corresponding `stream_begin`
envelope. Returns the view-id as a Maxima string. @var{kind} is a
renderer-side dispatch key (e.g. @code{ode_trajectory},
@code{table_append}, @code{mcmc_chain}).

emit_capabilities.summary: Emit a capabilities envelope announcing kernel version, lisp implementation, and supported features. @var{packages} is an optional Maxima list of strings naming packages the host wants to advertise.
emit_capabilities.body_md:
Emit a `capabilities` envelope announcing kernel version, lisp
implementation, and supported features. @var{packages} is an optional
Maxima list of strings naming packages the host wants to advertise.

emit_display.summary: Emit a display event with an explicit mime bundle. @var{pairs} is a Maxima list of [mime, payload] pairs. Used by libraries that know exactly what mime types they are producing (e.g. ax-plots emitting application/x-maxima-plotly).
emit_display.body_md:
Emit a `display` event with an explicit mime bundle. @var{pairs} is
a Maxima list of `[mime, payload]` pairs. Used by libraries that know
exactly what mime types they are producing (e.g. `ax-plots` emitting
`application/x-maxima-plotly`).

```
emit_display([
  ["application/x-maxima-plotly", json_string],
  ["text/plain", "<plot>"]
])$
```

emit_done.summary: Close a streaming view. @var{status} is one of the symbols @code{complete} (default), @code{cancelled}, @code{error}.
emit_done.body_md:
Close a streaming view. @var{status} is one of the symbols
@code{complete} (default), @code{cancelled}, @code{error}.

emit_error.summary: Emit a structured error envelope. @var{kind} is one of the symbols @code{maxima_error}, @code{lisp_error}, @code{parser_error}, @code{timeout}, @code{cancelled}. @var{message} is a string. The package auto-emits this envelope for eval-time failures; call @code{emit_error} explicitly only for cases the kernel doesn't catch (host-side timeouts, custom parser layers, …).

emit_frame.summary: Emit a frame envelope to a streaming view. @var{payload} shape is per-view-kind (e.g. [t, y] for an ODE trajectory).
emit_frame.body_md:
Emit a `frame` envelope to a streaming view. @var{payload} shape is
per-view-kind (e.g. `[t, y]` for an ODE trajectory).

emit_log.summary: Emit a log envelope attached to a view. @var{level} is one of the symbols @code{info}, @code{warn}, @code{error}.
emit_log.body_md:
Emit a `log` envelope attached to a view. @var{level} is one of the
symbols @code{info}, @code{warn}, @code{error}.

emit_progress.summary: Emit a progress envelope. @var{fraction} is a number between 0 and 1, or @code{none} (= null in JSON) if the total is unknown.
emit_progress.body_md:
Emit a `progress` envelope. @var{fraction} is a number between 0 and
1, or @code{none} (= `null` in JSON) if the total is unknown.

emit_ready.summary: Emit a ready envelope signalling the kernel will accept the next evaluation.
emit_ready.body_md:
Emit a `ready` envelope signalling the kernel will accept the next
evaluation.

emit_stdin_request.summary: Announce the kernel is blocking on user input. @var{prompt} is the text to display in the host UI. @var{kind} is one of the symbols @code{string}, @code{expression}, @code{debugger_command}. Returns the request id string so the caller can correlate the eventual response (delivered out-of-band — the events channel is one-way).
emit_stdin_request.body_md:
Announce the kernel is blocking on user input. @var{prompt} is the
text to display in the host UI. @var{kind} is one of the symbols
@code{string}, @code{expression}, @code{debugger_command}. Returns
the request id string so the caller can correlate the eventual
response (delivered out-of-band — the events channel is one-way).

emit_vars.summary: Snapshot @code{values} and emit a vars envelope carrying parallel arrays of variable names and their mgrind-rendered values.
emit_vars.body_md:
Snapshot @code{values} and emit a `vars` envelope carrying parallel
arrays of variable names and their mgrind-rendered values.

kernel_events_available.summary: Feature-detect. Returns @code{true} if the package is loaded — mirrors Python's from __future__ import … idiom for libraries that want to conditionally opt in.
kernel_events_available.body_md:
Feature-detect. Returns @code{true} if the package is loaded — mirrors
Python's `from __future__ import …` idiom for libraries that want to
conditionally opt in.

show.summary: Emit a display event for @var{expr} with a mime bundle (text/plain, application/x-maxima-latex, and others depending on the host's declared capabilities). The renderer picks the richest mime it can render.
show.body_md:
Emit a `display` event for @var{expr} with a mime bundle (text/plain,
application/x-maxima-latex, and others depending on the host's
declared capabilities). The renderer picks the richest mime it can
render.

```
for i:1 thru 3 do show(integrate(sin(i*x), x))$
```

examples.input: for i:1 thru 3 do show(integrate(sin(i*x), x))$

start_session.summary: Convenience: emit capabilities then ready in sequence — the standard session-start handshake. @var{packages} is forwarded to @code{emit_capabilities}.

sections.title: Introduction to kernel-events
sections.body_md:
Structured kernel-event channel for embedding Maxima in hosts. Emits
typed JSON envelopes (evaluation lifecycle, output, structured
display, errors, debugger events, streaming view frames, …) to a
host-registered sink. Embedding hosts consume the envelope stream and
expose it through their own protocols (HTTP/SSE for `maxima_mcp`,
fd-3 for `aximar`, …).

The package is pre-alpha. The envelope grammar will evolve through
v1.x as use cases land. See the design suite in `doc/design/` for the
architecture, prior-art references, and migration path from sentinel-
based stdout parsing.

To use the package:

```
load("kernel-events");
```

By itself the package emits envelopes only when a host has registered
a sink. Without a sink, all emission is a no-op — safe to load in any
context.

sections.title: See also
sections.body_md:
- `doc/design/kernel-events.md` — full envelope catalogue and design
- `doc/design/streaming.md` — streaming-specific envelopes and PoC
- `schemas/envelopes/v1/` — JSON Schema for each envelope type
